zh.lifeSummary: Muriel De Braose是William de Braose IV和Matilda de Clare的女兒，出生在英國巴金哈姆爾維可姆。她於1220年與Matthew I de Louvain在英國英格蘭艾可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1271年10月29日在英國素福爾可，埋葬在英國素福爾可。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Braose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Eckhard, Gerhard, Manfred, Otto.
from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Ambrosius (see Ambrose ).

variants.name: Ambrose
meanings: English and Scottish: from the personal name Ambrose (French Ambroise, Latin Ambrosius, from Greek ambrosios ‘immortal, divine’), which was popular throughout Christendom in medieval Europe. Its popularity was due in part to the fame of Saint Ambrose (c. 340–397), one of the four Latin Fathers of the Church, the teacher of Saint Augustine. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian Ambrož (see Ambroz ), and also their derivatives, e.g. the Slovenian patronymic Ambrožič.
Irish: from Mac Ambróis ‘son of Ambrose’ (see 1 above); a West Munster name, which has also been Anglicized as McCambridge .

nameMeanings.name: Muriel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Celtic origin ( see Muireall ). Forms of the name are found in Breton as well as in Irish and Scottish Gaelic, and in the form Meriel it was in use in the heart of England in the Middle Ages. The surname Merrill is derived from it. As Muriel it was at first used mainly in Scotland, but became more widely popular during the first half of the 20th century. Since then it has fallen from favour again.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Muriel De Braose was born in Wycombe, Buckinghamshire, England as the daughter of William de Braose IV and Matilda de Clare. She married Matthew I de Louvain in 1220, in Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 29 October 1271, in Suffolk, England, and was buried in Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Muriel De Braose est née à Wycombe, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, fille de William de Braose IV et Matilda de Clare. Elle a épousé Matthew I de Louvain en 1220, à Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 29 octobre 1271, à Suffolk, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Suffolk, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Muriel De BraoseはイギリスブクインガムシュアイアウイコンベでWilliam de Braose IVとMatilda de Clareの娘として生まれた。1220年にイギリスイギリスエセックスでMatthew I de Louvainと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1271年10月29日にイギリススフォルクで死去し，イギリススフォルクにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Muriel De Braose(은)는 영국 벅잉함실르 윈이코브에서 William de Braose IV(와)과 Matilda de Clare의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1220년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 Matthew I de Louvain(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1271년 10월 29일 영국 수폴크에서 사망했으며 영국 수폴크에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Muriel De Braose родилась в местности викомб, буккингхамшир, Англия. Её родителей звали William de Braose IV и Matilda de Clare. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Matthew I de Louvain в 1220 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 29 октября 1271 г. в местности суффолк, Англия и была похоронена в местности суффолк, Англия.
